citations.narrative:
The bamboo screening covering the bottom half of the Toucan enclosure was observed with jagged and frayed edges.
The bird was able to access the bamboo material and could possibly become ill if the material was ingested. In addition,
the bird could become injured due to the sharp bamboo edges. The facility must ensure that enclosures are kept in good
repair in order to protect the birds from injury or illness. The licensee must correct this issue.
********Correct by March 10, 2026

citations.desc: Environment enhancement to promote psychological well-being.
citations.narrative:
At the time of inspection, the facility was keeping the singly housed emu in an indoor space with hay for bedding, food,
water, and no outdoor access for viewing other birds or the outdoors during the winter months. The facility does not
provide the bird any environmental enrichment items or activities. According to the facility’s Avian Enrichment Plan, the
emu should have access to daily enrichment of seeing and hearing other birds and animals. Birds which do not have the
opportunity to engage in species typical behavior can be negatively impacted, causing psychological distress to their
health and well-being. The physical environment in primary enclosures must be enriched by materials or activities that
would provide the bird with the means to express non-injurious species- typical activities.
*******Correct by March 10, 2026

citations.desc: Environment enhancement to promote psychological well-being.
citations.narrative:
At the time of inspection, USDA observed that the facility has been housing the solitary emu indoors individually during
the winter months, without the ability to see or hear other birds. The facility’s Avian Enrichment plan states that all birds
are either group housed or they have the ability to see or hear other birds. The plan does not make any provisions for a
bird when it is individually housed in the winter enclosure. Birds which are individually housed that are unable to see or
hear birds of their own or compatible species can be negatively impacted regarding their health and well-being, causing
unnecessary psychological distress. These birds require special attention regarding enhancement of their environment,
and must be addressed on the Avian Enrichment plan. Exhibitors must develop, document, and follow a species
appropriate plan for environmental enhancement to promote the psychological well being of the birds that is within
industry standards and approved by the veterinarian. Additionally, this
plan must address the special considerations for the single housed emu that does not have access to hear and see other
bird species. The plan must be made available for future inspections.
*********Correct by March 15, 2026
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with the licensee.
n

narrative:
3.150(a) 
   
Facilities, general. 
The bamboo screening covering the bottom half of the Toucan enclosure was observed with jagged and frayed edges. 
The bird was able to access the bamboo material and could possibly become ill if the material was ingested. In addition, 
the bird could become injured due to the sharp bamboo edges. The facility must ensure that enclosures are kept in good 
repair in order to protect the birds from injury or illness. The licensee must correct this issue.  
********Correct by March 10, 2026 
3.154(b) 
   
Environment enhancement to promote psychological well-being. 
At the time of inspection, the facility was keeping the singly housed emu in an indoor space with hay for bedding, food, 
water, and no outdoor access for viewing other birds or the outdoors during the winter months. The facility does not 
provide the bird any environmental enrichment items or activities. According to the facility’s Avian Enrichment Plan, the 
emu should have access to daily enrichment of seeing and hearing other birds and animals. Birds which do not have the 
opportunity to engage in species typical behavior can be negatively impacted, causing psychological distress to their 
health and well-being. The physical environment in primary enclosures must be enriched by materials or activities that 
would provide the bird with the means to express non-injurious species- typical activities. 
*******Correct by March 10, 2026 
3.154(c)(4) 
   
Environment enhancement to promote psychological well-being. 
At the time of inspection, USDA observed that the facility has been housing the solitary emu indoors individually during 
the winter months, without the ability to see or hear other birds. The facility’s Avian Enrichment plan states that all birds 
are either group housed or they have the ability to see or hear other birds. The plan does not make any provisions for a 
bird when it is individually housed in the winter enclosure. Birds which are individually housed that are unable to see or 
hear birds of their own or compatible species can be negatively impacted regarding their health and well-being, causing 
unnecessary psychological distress. These birds require special attention regarding enhancement of their environment, 
and must be addressed on the Avian Enrichment plan. Exhibitors must develop, document, and follow a species 
appropriate plan for environmental enhancement to promote the psychological well being of the birds that is within 
industry standards and approved by the veterinarian. Additionally, this

plan must address the special considerations for the single housed emu that does not have access to hear and see other 
bird species. The plan must be made available for future inspections. 
*********Correct by March 15, 2026 
 
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with the licensee.
